How old is a book or source.

Need to assess sources strengths and weaknesses.

Who wrote it

What is it about

When was it written

What is the aim of the text

How?

Why?

What if?

How was the research conducted?

How might one factor impact another?

Why are these theories discussed?

![](data:image/png;base64,)

From < [https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/](https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/)>

From < [https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/](https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/)>

From < [https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/](https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/)>

![](data:image/png;base64,)

From < [https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/](https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/)>

Sources

Downsides

Critical Thinking Checklist        

✓ or ✗

Have I presented an argument?          

Have I justified my argument?          

Have I explained its significance?         

Is my argument convincing?         

Have I connected my ideas with appropriate signalling language?          

Are my ideas supported with evidence?         

Have I engaged critically with scholarly resources, evaluating the validity, coherence, and usefulness of their arguments?         

Have I considered the strengths and limitations of their positions?        

Have I considered numerous perspectives, developing a balanced argument?        

Have I engaged in comparative analysis, exploring relevant disparities, contradictions, or alternatives proposed by the wider academic community?         

Have I effectively refuted their findings and / or explained why they should be regarded as less important or contextually relevant?         

Is my analysis of those contrasting positions accurate and backed up with additional evidence?         

Have I discussed the extent to which these concepts, theories, or models can be applied to my organisation?         

Have I included examples?         

Have I proposed reasonable conclusions and / or recommendations?         

Does my argument progress logically and sequentially from introduction to conclusion?
